skill.name: ariadne-loop
skill.description: Create verifiable loop specifications, handoff packets, and supervision rules for AI coding agents.
skill.summary: Turn vague coding-agent work into bounded, testable loop contracts.
skill.author: zhangzeyu99-web

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged Markdown backticks, CLI examples, public URLs, and ordinary text as possible security issues. Review found no prompt injection, no executable bundled scripts, and no evidence of credential access or data exfiltration. The remaining risk is low because the skill may suggest running an external CLI when the user has installed it.

low_findings.title: Documented CLI Commands Require Normal Caution
low_findings.description: The static external command findings are Markdown examples and inline field names, not hidden executable code. The CLI examples can create local output files when a user chooses to run them, so they remain a low operational risk.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file contains only Markdown instructions and command examples. The examples are legitimate for the skill purpose, but external command execution still depends on user and agent behavior.

low_findings.title: Public Project URLs
low_findings.description: The network findings are hardcoded links to the project homepage and a browser-based playground. No evidence found that the skill sends local data, credentials, or telemetry to these URLs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both locations are static documentation links. There is no code path that performs a request or transmits user data.

low_findings.title: Static Blocker Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm and system reconnaissance flags are caused by ordinary prose and Markdown content. No evidence found of cryptographic code, host enumeration, or reconnaissance behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found natural-language instructions at these locations, not security-sensitive implementation. The skill has no executable source file beyond the Markdown skill definition.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Command Suggestions
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes examples for running the ariadne-loop CLI. These examples are transparent and relevant, but agents should not run them without normal user authorization and local context.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are explicit Markdown examples, not concealed execution. Risk depends on whether an agent runs them automatically in a user workspace.

content.value_statement: Vague coding-agent tasks often drift, repeat work, or stop without proof. This skill turns requests into bounded loop specs with verifiers, stop rules, and handoff packets.
actual_capabilities: Builds snapshot JSON structures for coding-agent tasks.
Defines inspect, act, verify, and decide cycles for agent work.
Creates verifier rules, stop rules, rollback behavior, and human gates.
Converts issue-style requests into executable loop specifications.
Provides JSON-only report contracts for repeated agent status updates.
Supports CLI-assisted workflows when ariadne-loop is already installed.
limitations: Does not install or verify the ariadne-loop CLI by itself.
Does not execute code, commit changes, deploy, or publish packages.
Requires accurate project context to produce useful verifiers.
Cannot prove remote state unless the agent reads back the target.

use_cases.title: Prepare a Bugfix Loop
use_cases.description: Turn a vague bug report into a loop with reproduction steps, verification commands, and rollback rules.

use_cases.title: Create an Agent Handoff
use_cases.description: Summarize current state, recent progress, constraints, and next verifiers for a resumable coding-agent task.

use_cases.title: Supervise Repeated Agent Reports
use_cases.description: Define report fields and decision rules for agents that must continue, stop, request review, or roll back.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Loop
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with a short task description.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an Ariadne Loop spec for this task: [describe the coding task]. Include goal, current state, verifiers, risks, and stop rules.

prompt_templates.title: Convert an Issue
prompt_templates.scenario: GitHub issue or ticket needs an executable agent task.
prompt_templates.prompt: Convert this issue into an agent loop spec. Use the title, body, acceptance criteria, verifiers, external effects, and human gates: [paste issue].

prompt_templates.title: Write a Handoff Packet
prompt_templates.scenario: A coding agent needs to resume existing work.
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a resumable handoff packet from this thread. Include current state, recent progress, constraints, open risks, concrete next step, and verification gates.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Supervised Loop
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced workflow with repeated reports and decision rules.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a supervised loop for this project task. Include report contract, continue conditions, stop conditions, rollback triggers, human approval gates, and readback requirements.

output_examples.input: A user asks an agent to fix a failing checkout test.
output_examples.output: A loop packet with the failing test as evidence, a reproduction verifier, allowed code areas, rollback rules, and a final passing-test gate.

output_examples.input: A team needs to resume a refactor after a long chat thread.
output_examples.output: A handoff summary with current state, completed changes, constraints, risks, next action, and clear verification steps.

output_examples.input: An operator wants repeated agent progress reports.
output_examples.output: A report contract that separates observed evidence, next action, passed gates, failed gates, and decisions that need human review.

best_practices: Provide concrete project context, failing outputs, and acceptance criteria before asking for a loop.
Use observable verifiers such as test output, artifact validation, screenshots, or remote readback.
Require human approval for commits, pushes, deploys, releases, deletions, and external messages.
anti_patterns: Do not accept vague gates such as done, looks good, or agent says it completed.
Do not let an agent perform external effects without explicit human confirmation.
Do not create long prompts when a smaller loop contract with verifiers is enough.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates loop specifications, handoff packets, verifier rules, and report contracts for AI coding-agent work.

faq.question: Does it require the ariadne-loop CLI?
faq.answer: No. The skill can write the structures directly, but it can use CLI workflows when the CLI is installed.

faq.question: Can it run commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It documents possible CLI commands and should rely on user approval before any external effect.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: It is written for Claude, Codex, Claude Code, OpenClaw, and similar AI coding agents.

faq.question: What makes a good verifier?
faq.answer: A good verifier is observable, repeatable, and tied to evidence such as command output or validated artifacts.

faq.question: When should I use a human gate?
faq.answer: Use a human gate before commits, pushes, deploys, releases, deletion, package publishing, billing, or external messages.
